summary: Verified server.py runs without .env via safe defaults and `python server.py` entrypoint. Confirmed .env.example exists with all required keys. Confirmed existing supervisor-backed API + auth + journals still work after restoring .env.
passed: .env.example exists at /app/backend/.env.example with MONGO_URL, DB_NAME, CORS_ORIGINS, FRONTEND_URL, JWT_SECRET, ADMIN_EMAIL, ADMIN_PASSWORD as real key=value lines
server.py has DEFAULTS dict + os.environ.setdefault loop for all 7 required keys (MONGO_URL, DB_NAME, JWT_SECRET, ADMIN_EMAIL, ADMIN_PASSWORD, FRONTEND_URL, CORS_ORIGINS)
server.py has `if __name__ == "__main__": uvicorn.run(...)` entrypoint honoring PORT/HOST env vars
No-env boot: moved /app/backend/.env to /tmp/env.bak, ran `PORT=8123 python server.py`, GET http://localhost:8123/api/ returned 200 with {"message":"Trade Journal API","status":"ok"}
Cleanup done: process killed, port 8123 free, /app/backend/.env restored, supervisor backend restarted
Existing behavior intact: GET /api/ -> 200, POST /api/auth/login (admin@tradejournal.com/Admin@123) -> 200 with cookies, GET /api/auth/me -> 200, GET /api/journals -> 200 with existing TSLA journal
notes: Frontend testing skipped per request. .env.example uses quoted values (KEY="value") which python-dotenv parses correctly; keys are real (not commented placeholders).
